title: Lead AI/Data Science Engineer
company_focus: Global healthcare technology and medical devices
description:
At Medtronic you can begin a life-long career of exploration
and innovation, while helping champion healthcare access and
equity for all. You’ll lead with purpose, breaking down
barriers to innovation in a more connected, compassionate
world.

A Day in the LifeAt Medtronic, we’re pioneering the use of
AI and computer vision to revolutionize healthcare. Our work
drives the development of advanced medical devices by
transforming image data into actionable insights. We are
seeking a Lead Data Scientist specializing in computer
vision to lead the creation of cutting-edge AI models,
unlocking new possibilities for patient care and clinical
outcomes.

Responsibilities may include the following and other duties
may be assigned

- Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including
  radiologists, clinical experts, and product managers, to
  define AI-powered solutions in the medical imaging domain.
- Lead the design and execution of advanced computer vision
  pipelines for tasks like image segmentation, object
  detection, classification, and anomaly detection using
  medical images (CT, MRI, X-ray, ultrasound).
- Drive preprocessing of large-scale medical image datasets,
  addressing noise reduction, normalization, and other
  domain-specific challenges.
- Research, design, and implement state-of-the-art deep
  learning algorithms using frameworks like TensorFlow,
  PyTorch, or equivalent for medical imaging use cases.
- Ensure scalability and efficiency of AI models by optimizing
  them for deployment on cloud and edge devices.
- Perform rigorous validation and testing of models to ensure
  they meet regulatory and clinical standards for safety and
  accuracy.
- Mentor a team of data scientists and engineers, providing
  technical guidance and fostering an environment of
  innovation.
- Stay at the forefront of advancements in computer vision and
  medical imaging, integrating cutting-edge techniques into
  product development.
- Publish and present research findings in internal forums or
  relevant conferences, contributing to the company’s
  thought leadership in AI and healthcare.

Required Knowledge and Experience

- 10+ years of experience in data science or AI, with at least
  6+ years focused on computer vision applications.
- Proven expertise in medical imaging analysis, including
  working with modalities such as CT, MRI, X-ray, and
  ultrasound.
- Advanced proficiency in Python and deep learning frameworks
  (TensorFlow, PyTorch).
- Deep understanding of computer vision techniques, including
  image segmentation, feature extraction, object detection,
  and generative models.
- Experience in handling large-scale image datasets, including
  data wrangling, augmentation, and preprocessing.
- Strong knowledge of DICOM formats, PACS systems, and medical
  data compliance standards.
- Ability to work within regulated environments and
  familiarity with FDA/CE requirements for AI in medical
  devices.
- Excellent communication skills to convey complex AI concepts
  to non-technical stakeholders and clinical collaborators.
- B.Tech / Masters / Ph.D. in Computer Science, Biomedical
  Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field
  with a focus on computer vision or AI.
- 10+ years of professional experience in data science, AI, or
  machine learning roles, with a track record of leadership
  in complex projects.

Physical Job Requirements The above statements are intended
to describe the general nature and level of work being
performed by employees assigned to this position, but they
are not an exhaustive list of all the required
responsibilities and skills of this position.

Benefits & Compensation

Medtronic offers a competitive Salary and flexible Benefits
Package A commitment to our employees lives at the core of
our values. We recognize their contributions. They share in
the success they help to create.  We offer a wide range of
benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans
designed to support you at every career and life stage.


This position is eligible for a short-term incentive called
the Medtronic Incentive Plan (MIP).

About Medtronic

We lead global healthcare technology and boldly attack the
most challenging health problems facing humanity by
searching out and finding solutions. Our Mission — to
alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life — unites a
global team of 95,000+ passionate people. We are engineers
at heart— putting ambitious ideas to work to generate real
solutions for real people. From the R&D lab, to the factory
floor, to the conference room, every one of us experiments,
creates, builds, improves and solves. We have the talent,
diverse perspectives, and guts to engineer the
extraordinary.

Learn more about our business, mission, and our commitment
to diversity here
responsibilities: Collaborate with radiologists, clinical experts, and product managers to define AI-powered solutions for medical imaging
Lead the design and execution of advanced computer vision pipelines for image segmentation, object detection, classification, and anomaly detection using medical images
Drive preprocessing of large-scale medical image datasets including noise reduction and normalization
Research, design, and implement state-of-the-art deep learning algorithms using TensorFlow, PyTorch for medical imaging use cases
Optimize AI models for deployment on cloud and edge devices ensuring scalability and efficiency
Perform rigorous validation and testing of models to meet regulatory and clinical standards for safety and accuracy
Mentor a team of data scientists and engineers, providing technical guidance and fostering innovation
Publish and present research findings in internal forums and relevant conferences
use_cases: Medical image segmentation for CT, MRI, X-ray, and ultrasound scans
Object detection and anomaly detection in medical imaging
Classification of medical images for clinical diagnosis support
Transforming medical image data into actionable clinical insights
AI-powered medical device development for improved patient care and clinical outcomes
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is directly focused on building and deploying
AI/computer vision models for medical imaging. The lead will
design and implement deep learning algorithms, create
computer vision pipelines for image segmentation, object
detection, and classification, and optimize models for
deployment on cloud and edge devices. This is hands-on AI
model development work.
